title: A computational rule-based model of MAPK/ERK system regulation
task: Model MAPK/ERK pathway isoform-specific regulation and graded vs switch-like response integration
key_results: Rule-based model reconciles graded RAS/RAF response with switch-like ERK response via spatially restricted RAS activation; 14-3-3 dimers inhibit RAF monomers and preferentially stabilize RAF heterodimers containing BRAF; functional differences between BRAF/CRAF/ARAF arise from isoform-specific 14-3-3 interactions
comments: The rule-based approach elegantly captures pathway complexity across isoforms and scaffolds, though parameter uncertainty and cell-line-specific variations suggest the nominal model requires extensive validation against quantitative time-course data.

model_io.model: Rule-based MAPK/ERK pathway model (BioNetGen-style)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Spatially restricted RAS activation at low growth factor concentration generates graded response at RAS/RAF but switch-like response at ERK
hypotheses.result: Supported: model reconciles observed response signatures across cascade tiers

hypotheses.hypothesis: 14-3-3 binding selectively stabilizes RAF heterodimers (BRAF-CRAF/ARAF) but inhibits RAF monomers
hypotheses.result: Proposed mechanism; creates isoform-specific functional differences and cell-line heterogeneity

title: Understanding the underlying language code that governs the π–π non-covalent interactions between proteins and DNA
task: Model aromatic π–π interactions between DNA bases and protein aromatic amino acids via quantum mechanics
key_results: Optimal dimer geometry involves parallel displacement of benzene ring; pz orbitals of N3/C2 (purines) and C5/C6 (pyrimidines) couple with C1/C2 carbons of aromatic rings; Hellmann–Feynman theorem adapted to estimate interaction force
comments: The quantum orbital-level analysis is theoretically novel, but lacks experimental validation through mutagenesis or structural evidence of the predicted geometries in protein–DNA complexes.

model_io.model: Quantum mechanical dimer model (electron pairs in oscillatory resonant states)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Parallel displacement geometry is most stable for aromatic base-amino acid coupling
hypotheses.result: Predicted by quantum model; specific orbital overlaps (N3/C2 and C5/C6 with C1/C2 carbons) identified

title: Quasi-elastic neutron scattering studies on bacterial spores and their hydration water
task: Elucidate water dynamics and protein immobilization in dormant bacterial spores
key_results: Proteome shows strongly reduced rotational dynamics; water molecules and small molecular subgroups retain high mobility at sub-nanosecond timescale; reduced hydration paired with high water mobility in dormant state
comments: The neutron scattering subtraction procedure is technically sophisticated, but the functional significance of the mobility discrepancy between proteome and hydration water for spore viability remains speculative.

model_io.model: Quasi-elastic neutron scattering (QENS) with spectral subtraction

hypotheses.hypothesis: High water mobility combined with protein immobilization supports spore dormancy and rapid germination response
hypotheses.result: Supported indirectly: proteome shows reduced dynamics while water retains mobility at sub-nanosecond scale

title: Unstructured, disulfide-bridged C-terminus in helminth α-helical antimicrobial peptides enhances and modulates their activity
task: Characterize structural determinants controlling antimicrobial peptide activity via disulfide-bridged C-terminal tail
key_results: Disulfide-bridged mesco-2 forms oligomeric assemblies with faster membrane disruption; reduced analogue mesco-2 A binds as monomers with slower kinetics; C-terminal cyclic tail tunes cooperativity and speed of activity
comments: The systematic decomposition of peptide function into secondary structure, oligomerization state, and membrane interaction modes provides a clear engineering principle, though the molecular determinants of oligomer selectivity remain partially unresolved.

model_io.model: Molecular modelling + AFM + Flow cytometry
inputs: mesco-2 peptide (helical GRGIGRG motif + disulfide CLGRC loop)

hypotheses.hypothesis: C-terminal disulfide bridge modulates peptide flexibility and oligomerization state
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: disulfide-bridged mesco-2 forms assemblies; reduced mesco-2 A binds as monomers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Distinct oligomerization states produce different membrane interaction modes and kinetics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: mesco-2 (assembly) shows faster membrane disruption; mesco-2 A (monomer) shows slower kinetics

title: Lactylation stabilizes PD-L1 to promote tumor immune evasion and cell growth
task: Determine how lactate stabilizes PD-L1 to promote tumor immune evasion
key_results: Lactate induces K280 lactylation of PD-L1 via AARS1; lactylation inhibits HUWE1-mediated ubiquitination and degradation; PD-L1 K280 lactylation correlates with advanced NSCLC stages and poor survival
comments: Novel metabolic-epigenetic axis linking lactate to immune checkpoint function; therapeutic potential in augmenting anti-PD-L1 blockade.

model_io.model: Tumor cells with lactate/PD-L1 signaling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lactate-mediated PD-L1 lactylation promotes tumor immune evasion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; lactylation stabilizes PD-L1 and impairs CD8+ cytotoxicity

hypotheses.hypothesis: AARS1 catalyzes PD-L1 lactylation using lactate as donor
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; AARS1 identified as lactyltransferase for K280

title: TSP-1 interaction with RANK and OPG: implications for bone remodeling and osteolytic bone metastasis
task: Elucidate TSP-1 role in controlling osteoclast differentiation and bone metastasis-associated osteolysis
key_results: TSP-1 C-terminal fragment E123CaG binds RANK and impairs RANKL signaling (p38, JNK, NFATc1); E123CaG also stabilizes OPG; in vivo expression of E123CaG reduces osteolytic lesions and prolongs survival
comments: Dual mechanism (RANK inhibition + OPG stabilization) is elegant but depends on HTRA1-mediated TSP-1 cleavage feedback.

model_io.model: Osteoclast differentiation and bone remodeling

hypotheses.hypothesis: TSP-1 C-terminal fragment inhibits RANKL-induced osteoclastogenesis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; E123CaG impairs p38, JNK, and NFATc1 signaling

hypotheses.hypothesis: E123CaG potentiates OPG by protecting it from protease degradation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; E123CaG binding enhances OPG inhibitory activity

title: An epithelial morphogenetic program for maximal urine concentration
task: Identify morphogenetic program in ascending thin limb for maximal urine concentration
key_results: Extensive apical-junctional interdigitations in aTL increase cell-cell contact; claudin-10b drives interdigitation formation; claudin-10b deletion abolishes interdigitations and reduces urine-concentrating ability; claudin-10b acts via ZO1 interaction and transcellular adhesion
comments: Elegant link between epithelial morphology and organ-level function; claudin-10b's morphogenetic role is non-canonical and distinct from typical tight junction cation permeability.

model_io.model: Ascending thin limb epithelial morphogenesis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Claudin-10b drives apical-junctional interdigitation formation in aTL
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; claudin-10b deletion abolishes interdigitations

hypotheses.hypothesis: aTL interdigitations are essential for maximal urinary concentration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; claudin-10b deletion markedly reduces urine-concentrating ability

title: Validation of ferroptosis in zebrafish as a reliable model for phenotypic studies
task: Validate ferroptosis in zebrafish larvae as a reliable phenotypic disease model alternative to mice
key_results: Ferroptosis inducers (Erastin, FIN56, FAC) successfully induce ferroptosis in zebrafish; ROS elevation, GSH/GSSG ratio reduction, and differential ferroptosis gene/protein expression confirmed; ferroptosis inhibits vascular development in transgenic zebrafish
comments: Zebrafish provides efficient alternative to mice (85% genetic homology, transparent larvae); however, mechanistic depth may still require mammalian validation.

model_io.model: Ferroptosis in zebrafish embryos and larvae

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ferroptosis model can be established in zebrafish using inducers
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Erastin, FIN56, and FAC induce ferroptosis markers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ferroptosis significantly inhibits angiogenesis in zebrafish
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ferroptosis-induced vascular development impairment observed

title: SHP2 improves ovarian morphology and steroidogenic function in a rat PCOS model by modulating IRE1α/XBP1/NLRP3-mediated granulosa cell pyroptosis
task: Elucidate SHP2 mechanism in ameliorating PCOS by modulating granulosa cell pyroptosis via IRE1α/XBP1/NLRP3 pathway
key_results: SHP2 overexpression reduces PCOS-induced ovarian damage, cystic follicles, and normalizes hormone levels; SHP2 suppresses testosterone-activated IRE1α/XBP1/NLRP3 and ZEB1 pathways; SHP2 inhibits GSDMD and pyroptosis while promoting PKP3 and proliferation
comments: Integrates endoplasmic reticulum stress, inflammasome, and epithelial-mesenchymal pathways; SHP2 phosphorylation status is testosterone-sensitive, suggesting metabolic-epigenetic coupling.

model_io.model: PCOS granulosa cell pyroptosis regulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: SHP2 overexpression ameliorates PCOS-induced ovarian damage and hormone abnormalities
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SHP2-OE reduces cystic follicles and normalizes estradiol, testosterone, and gonadotropin levels

hypotheses.hypothesis: SHP2 suppresses testosterone-activated IRE1α/XBP1/NLRP3 pathway to inhibit pyroptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; testosterone activates pathway; SHP2 downregulates p-IRE1α, XBP1s, and NLRP3

title: Hibernating bear serum triggers an anti-fibrotic signature in human fibroblasts, involving ECM remodeling and MAPK signaling activation
task: Identify anti-fibrotic mechanisms triggered by hibernating bear serum in human fibroblasts
key_results: Hibernating bear serum reduces fibrotic markers (COL2A1, COL11A1, COL9A1, TGFBI) and inflammatory markers (IFI16, HMGB2, HMGB); activates MAPK cascade; demonstrates anti-fibrotic ECM remodeling signature
comments: Novel translational approach using bear hibernation physiology; proteomics-driven discovery but specific circulating factors remain unidentified.

model_io.model: Human fibroblasts treated with hibernating bear serum

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hibernating bear serum induces anti-fibrotic signature in human fibroblasts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; reduced collagen and TGFBI levels, increased MAPK activation, and decreased inflammatory markers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hibernation-related circulating factors modulate pro-fibrosis pathways and ECM homeostasis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; hibernating bear serum triggers ECM remodeling and anti-fibrotic gene expression programs

title: Targeting STAT3 pathway attenuates macrophages inflammation and cardiovascular injury in a model of Kawasaki disease
task: Determine STAT3 pathway role in macrophage-mediated inflammation and cardiovascular injury in Kawasaki disease
key_results: STAT3 phosphorylation elevated in LCWE-treated macrophages; STAT3 inhibition by AG490 reduces inflammatory cytokine production and endothelial cell damage; AG490 treatment mitigates cardiac inflammation and vascular injury in LCWE-KD mouse model
comments: Straightforward in vitro-to-in vivo validation; STAT3 as promising KD therapeutic target but mechanism details (upstream LCWE sensors) remain to be explored.

model_io.model: LCWE-induced Kawasaki disease vasculitis model

hypotheses.hypothesis: STAT3 blockade inhibits LCWE-mediated macrophage inflammatory response
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; AG490 significantly reduces inflammation in RAW264.7 and primary macrophages

hypotheses.hypothesis: STAT3 inhibition attenuates endothelial injury in KD model
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; AG490 mitigates coronary endothelial damage and vascular injury in vivo

title: mRNA profiling of mesenchymal stem cell-derived exosomes reveals their function in accelerating wound healing
task: Profile UCMSC-derived exosomal transcriptome and validate functional roles in cutaneous wound healing
key_results: 4,578 protein-coding genes detected in exosomes; 2,004 upregulated, 2,574 downregulated vs. parental cells; wound-healing genes enriched in DNA replication, ribosome, cell cycle, and pyrimidine pathways; UCMSC-EX treatment accelerates wound closure in burned mice
comments: Transcriptomic profiling reveals wound-healing enrichment; functional validation limited to fibroblast/keratinocyte proliferation and early-stage closure.

model_io.model: UCMSC-derived exosome-mediated wound healing

hypotheses.hypothesis: UCMSC-derived exosomes are enriched for wound-healing-related genes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; upregulated genes associated with DNA replication, ribosome, cell cycle, and pyrimidine metabolism

hypotheses.hypothesis: UCMSC-EX treatment promotes fibroblast and keratinocyte proliferation and accelerates wound closure in vivo
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; UCMSC-EX efficiently penetrate dermis, promote cell proliferation, and accelerate early-stage wound closure

title: β-arrestin1 orchestrates endosomal signaling to regulate translational control of circadian light entrainment
task: Determine β-arrestin1 role in endosomal signaling for circadian light entrainment via translational control
key_results: β-arrestin1 required for PAC1 receptor internalization and endosomal signaling activation; endosomal ERK1/2-RSK1-S6 cascade enhances protein translation and PER induction; transcriptional responses remain intact, demonstrating spatial specificity
comments: Reveals endosomes as critical circadian signal hubs; non-canonical GPCR signaling mechanism challenges traditional paradigms.

model_io.model: Circadian light entrainment via endosomal signaling

hypotheses.hypothesis: β-arrestin1 is required for PAC1 endosomal signaling in response to light/PACAP
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ARRB1 deletion impairs PAC1 internalization and endosomal signaling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Endosomal ERK1/2-RSK1-S6 cascade enhances PER translation and circadian entrainment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; endosomal signaling contributes to PER induction; transcription remains intact

title: Multi-omic identification of key transcriptional regulatory programs during endurance exercise training in rats
task: Multi-omic identification of transcriptional regulatory programs in endurance training
key_results: Integrated analysis of 5 omic layers across 8 tissues identified tissue-specific TF motif enrichment; distinct routes of training regulation via epigenomic access vs. TF expression/activity changes
comments: Comprehensive multi-omic integration; 50 rats with equal sex representation is solid; mechanistic insights into epigenomic vs. transcriptional routes are valuable for understanding training adaptation.

model_io.model: Multi-omic integration (chromatin accessibility, DNA methylation, mRNA, protein abundance/phosphorylation, motif enrichment)
inputs: tissues from 50 rats post-endurance training (equal sex)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Training-induced responses occur via epigenomic opening for TF access or via TF expression/activity changes
hypotheses.result: Identified distinct regulatory routes; tissue-specific TF motifs enriched in accessible/methylated regions and differentially expressed genes

title: Investigating the mechanistic link between pesticide DDT and breast cancer through network toxicology, molecular docking, and molecular dynamics simulation
task: DDT-breast cancer mechanistic pathway elucidation
key_results: 12 DDT-associated breast cancer targets identified; AR, ESR1, ESR2, ERBB2 identified as core nodes in hormone/growth factor signaling
comments: Network toxicology provides plausible mechanism but lacks experimental validation; molecular docking scores and binding affinities not reported quantitatively.

model_io.model: Network toxicology + Protein-protein interaction + Molecular docking

hypotheses.hypothesis: DDT interacts with hormone/growth factor signaling proteins to promote breast cancer
hypotheses.result: 12 targets identified with core involvement in AR/ESR/ERBB2 pathways; requires experimental validation

title: Feature reduction using swarm optimization and random forest classifiers for early diabetes risk prediction
task: Feature reduction and diabetes risk prediction via swarm optimization
key_results: TSO_RF (Tuna Swarm Optimization + Random Forest) achieved 100% accuracy, 98.14% 10-fold CV with 14/16 features; SHAP identified Polyuria, Polydipsia, Gender as top predictors
comments: Efficient feature reduction (14→16) with near-perfect accuracy on Early Stage Diabetes dataset; SHAP interpretation adds transparency; limited to small (n=520) single-dataset evaluation.

model_io.model: Fox Optimizer/Honey Badger Algorithm/Tuna Swarm Optimization + Random Forest
inputs: Early Stage Diabetes Risk Prediction dataset (16 features, 520 individuals)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Swarm-based feature selection + RF maintains high accuracy with minimal features
hypotheses.result: TSO_RF: 100% accuracy, 98.14% 10-fold CV with 14 features; HBA_RF: highest reduction to 10 features

title: A multi-scale adaptive filtering and AtRes_SRU–transformer synergy for breast cancer histopathology classification
task: Breast cancer histopathology image classification
key_results: ImTranNet-TriCore achieved 95.92% accuracy with 97.83% precision (Brain MRI), 97.67% (Chest X-ray), 93.75% recall (Brain MRI)
comments: Multi-component architecture appears effective but claims of superiority to CNNs/RNNs/Transformers are overstated without rigorous comparative benchmarking on identical data splits.

model_io.model: LM-AdaFilter + DP-AtRes-SRU + Multi-Head Hybrid Transformer (ImTranNet-TriCore)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Learnable multi-scale filtering + attentive residual SRU + hybrid transformer improve noise robustness
hypotheses.result: 95.92% accuracy with low false positive rate compared to conventional deep learning

title: A novel bioenergetic model outlines the metabolism of a deep-sea clam and that of its sulfur-oxidizing symbionts
task: Bioenergetic modeling of deep-sea chemosymbiotic clam and sulfur-oxidizing symbionts
key_results: Novel 'farming' DEB model revealed host maintains lower ingestion for stable symbiont populations; predicted high oxygen consumption by symbionts in adults and high host maintenance flux
comments: Innovative explicit modeling of host-symbiont digestion; unexpected homeostatic behavior is intriguing but based on model predictions requiring empirical validation of metabolic rates.

model_io.model: Dynamic Energy Budget (DEB) - Classical and 'Farming' variants

hypotheses.hypothesis: Host actively regulates ingestion to maintain stable symbiont populations (farming strategy)
hypotheses.result: Farming model predicts lower stable ingestion vs. maximal; symbiont oxygen consumption dominates in adults

title: Hybrid tuned deep learning model for breast cancer diagnosis using genetic data
task: Breast cancer diagnosis and prognosis from whole-genome data
key_results: CNN-BiLSTM hybrid model achieved 97.4% accuracy on TCGA and 99.30% on METABRIC with 100% recall for cancer mortality prediction
comments: Strong performance on both datasets is notable, but lacks rigorous external validation beyond METABRIC; feature selection combining RF and ARM is interesting but not deeply justified.

model_io.model: Random Forest + Association Rule Mining + CNN-BiLSTM

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-dimensional input with hybrid deep learning outperforms traditional gene set selection
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with 97.4% and 99.30% accuracy on two datasets

title: A novel spatiotemporal decomposition and identification of sparse equations for human brain deformation
task: Reduced-order modeling of brain deformation dynamics
key_results: TASC-DMD + SINDy identified sparse 3-mode description of brain deformation; exceptional dimensionality reduction with accurate prediction on held-out subjects (n=9)
comments: Novel DMD framework is elegant; validation on only 9 test subjects from 45-subject cohort is modest; generalization to pathological cases unclear.

model_io.model: Time-Augmented Space-Contracted DMD (TASC-DMD) + Sparse Identification of Nonlinear Dynamics (SINDy)
inputs: 4D strain data from tagged MRI (36 training subjects)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Time-delay embedding improves DMD efficiency for spatiotemporal brain dynamics
hypotheses.result: 3-mode sparse model accurately predicted deformation in 9 independent test subjects

title: Development and validation of an interpretable prediction model for the risk of unplanned reoperation in patients underwent intracranial tumor surgery
task: Machine learning prediction of unplanned reoperation after intracranial tumor surgery
key_results: Logistic regression model achieved AUC 0.836 (training) and 0.769 (internal test); surgery duration, tumor location, Frailty Index-5 identified as top predictors via SHAP
comments: Well-constructed with feature selection, internal validation, and SHAP interpretability; single-center cohort limits generalizability; web app aids clinical accessibility.

model_io.model: LASSO + Logistic Regression

hypotheses.hypothesis: Surgery duration, tumor location, and frailty are major independent predictors of reoperation
hypotheses.result: SHAP identified these as top drivers; LR model AUC 0.836/0.769 on training/test sets

title: Performance of a GPU- and time-efficient pseudo-3D network for magnetic resonance image super-resolution and motion artifact reduction
task: GPU-efficient 2D pseudo-3D framework for MRI super-resolution and artifact reduction
key_results: TS-RCAN outperformed 3D networks by >0.01 dB SSIM and 1.5 dB PSNR for ×2-4 acceleration while reducing GPU load 90%; uncertainty estimation correlated with quality metrics
comments: Practical efficiency gains are significant for clinical deployment; uncertainty quantification without ground truth is valuable; evaluation on single anatomical region.

model_io.model: TS-RCAN (Time/GPU-efficient 2D) pseudo-3D network

hypotheses.hypothesis: 2D pseudo-3D processing with uncertainty estimation achieves comparable/superior performance to 3D networks with lower resource cost
hypotheses.result: TS-RCAN exceeded 3D baselines in SSIM/PSNR while reducing GPU/inference time 90%

title: The most important features in generalized additive models might be groups of features
task: Feature importance assessment for groups in GAMs
key_results: Method enables efficient group importance ranking without retraining; identified multimodal feature groups as primary predictors in depression and surgical outcome datasets
comments: Addresses a genuine gap in interpretability for multimodal data, though validation is limited to two medical case studies.

model_io.model: Generalized Additive Models (GAMs)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Joint signals from feature groups are more predictive than individual features in multimodal datasets
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in depression symptoms and hip arthroplasty outcome datasets

title: Classification of fallers and non-fallers in older adults using electrical IMU signal for gait analysis and explainable deep learning
task: IMU-based gait analysis for fall-risk classification in older adults
key_results: LSTM/CNN achieved 95-96% accuracy (data-level CV); CNN maintained 92% under subject-held-out CV; stance phase irregularities accounted for 83.8% of faller classification via LIME
comments: Rigorous cross-validation and LIME interpretation strengthen claims; mechanistic insights (weight transfer, balance) align with fall neuromechanics but need prospective validation.

model_io.model: LSTM/CNN
inputs: 6-channel IMU (acceleration, angular velocity) stride time-series from 30-min walks

hypotheses.hypothesis: Terminal/mid-stance irregularities (weight transfer, balance) are primary gait drivers of fall-risk classification
hypotheses.result: LIME showed stance phase contributes 83.8% to faller classification; CNN 92% accuracy under held-out subject CV

title: Hair and artifact removal in dermoscopic images using deep learning for enhanced skin cancer detection
task: Hair and artifact removal from dermoscopic images for enhanced melanoma detection
key_results: U-Net preprocessing improved melanoma classification accuracy from 84.2% to 90.3-91.5%; PSNR 21.5→34.1 dB, SSIM 0.79→0.89 (HAM10000); IoU 0.85-0.87
comments: Strong quantitative improvements in image quality and downstream classification; extensive data augmentation and hybrid loss function well-motivated; generalization across artifact types demonstrated.

model_io.model: U-Net (Dice + BCE hybrid loss)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Artifact removal preserves lesion features and improves downstream melanoma classification
hypotheses.result: Accuracy 84.2%→90.3-91.5%, F1 81.6%→90.2-91.5%; PSNR/SSIM improved significantly

title: Q-CaDD: accelerating in silico methodologies with quantum computation and machine learning for Epidermal growth factor receptor
task: Hybrid quantum-classical drug discovery for EGFR targeting
key_results: Q-CaDD hybrid ensemble (QSVM + classical models) achieved AUC-ROC 0.83 on Tox21; identified candidate ligands with favorable affinity/toxicity profiles
comments: Interesting quantum-ML integration, but modest AUC and reliance on NISQ hardware limits immediate practical impact; experimental validation pending.

model_io.model: STONED-SELFIES + AutoDock Vina + Quantum SVM + Ensemble classical ML

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantum-enhanced components provide complementary signal in hybrid drug discovery workflows
hypotheses.result: QSVM ensemble achieved AUC 0.83, competitive with classical benchmarks on NR-AR endpoint

title: Construction of a diagnostic model for preeclampsia based on differentially expressed lactylation-related genes and the immune infiltration analysis
task: Preeclampsia diagnostic biomarker discovery via lactylation-related genes
key_results: Two LRDEGs (EAF1, PFKP) identified; logistic regression model achieved 81.7% on discovery set, 91% on GSE54618 validation
comments: Lactylation as disease mechanism is understudied; lack of independent cohort validation and mechanistic validation in cell/animal models limits impact.

model_io.model: DEG identification + Pathway enrichment + Logistic Regression + CIBERSORT + scRNA-seq

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lactylation-related genes regulate glycolysis pathway in preeclampsia
hypotheses.result: EAF1 and PFKP upregulation linked to Glycolysis/Gluconeogenesis pathway; confirmed in placental tissue

title: AI-based detection of Certas Plus shunt valve settings in CT scans
task: AI-based CSF shunt valve setting detection in CT imaging
key_results: 3D U-Net segmentation achieved 97.3% success rate; valve setting prediction 96% accuracy (exact or adjacent setting)
comments: Well-designed clinical application with clear utility; segmentation visualization aids clinician verification, but external validation on other valve types not addressed.

model_io.model: 3D U-Net
inputs: head CT scans with Certas Plus valves

hypotheses.hypothesis: Deep segmentation of radiopaque valve markers enables accurate setting inference
hypotheses.result: 97.3% segmentation success, 96% correct setting prediction

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence in intensive care units: from narrow tools to generalist systems
task: Regulatory framework for AI in intensive care units
key_results: Five-paradigm framework maps regulatory complexity vs. AI functionality/scale; agentic oversight proposed for orchestrating multi-AI systems
comments: Conceptual perspective on emerging regulatory needs; lacks empirical data on actual ICU AI deployment challenges and outcomes.

title: Evolutionary digital twin framework for optimal aminoglycoside dosing in neonates with suspected sepsis
task: Precision antibiotic dosing in neonatal sepsis via evolutionary digital twins
key_results: Physiologically-based framework with eco-evolutionary pharmacodynamics achieved bacteriostatic exposure across simulated cohorts; LSTM-updated GFR improves calibration on 1634 real neonates
comments: Multiscale integration of PK/PD with resistance dynamics is innovative; clinical deployment feasibility unclear due to real-time parameter estimation requirements.

model_io.model: Physiologically-based PK model + Eco-evolutionary PD + LSTM-GFR + Model Predictive Control

hypotheses.hypothesis: Evolution-aware dosing accounting for resistance emergence improves therapeutic outcome
hypotheses.result: MPC further reduced bacterial rebound during late therapy vs. nonlinear optimal control

title: High-sensitivity pan-cancer AI assessment of lymph node metastasis via uncertainty quantification
task: Pan-cancer lymph node metastasis assessment with uncertainty quantification
key_results: UPATHLN (pathology foundation model + uncertainty module) achieved AUC 0.986; 100% conditional sensitivity via flagged false negatives across 26,229 lymph nodes from 14 primaries; 73.2% reduction in negative node review burden
comments: Excellent safety-critical design; uncertainty as fail-safe for rare variants is novel; multi-centre, pan-cancer validation is robust; real-world workload reduction is practical.

model_io.model: Pathology foundation model encoder + Decoupled uncertainty estimation
inputs: histological slides from 26,229 lymph nodes (14 primary tumor origins)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit uncertainty estimation intercepts overconfident false negatives on rare tumor variants
hypotheses.result: 100% conditional sensitivity; 73.2% review burden reduction on negatives; generalized to 7 unseen primaries

title: Macroscopic particle transport in dissipative long-range bosonic systems
task: Determine maximal transport speed in dissipative bosonic systems with long-range interactions
key_results: Fundamental distinction between one-body and multi-body loss regimes; decoherence-free subspaces enable long-distance perfect transport
comments: Theoretically rigorous using optimal transport for open quantum systems, but experimental validation protocols need further development.

model_io.model: Dissipative bosonic lattice with long-range hopping and interactions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decoherence-free subspaces facilitate long-distance transport in dissipative systems
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; emergence of decoherence-free subspaces enables perfect transport regardless of loss

hypotheses.hypothesis: Arbitrarily small gain enables long-distance transport in dilute lattice gases
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; gain overcomes loss effects in sparse systems

title: Flat band induced quasi-one-dimensional magnon transport in a two-dimensional spin lattice
task: Demonstrate quasi-1D magnon transport via flat magnonic bands in 2D antiferromagnetic insulator
key_results: Magnon diffusion length exceeding 7 μm along a-axis (rivaling YIG); anisotropic transport explained by flat band along b-axis and dispersive band along a-axis
comments: First experimental demonstration of flat-band enhanced magnon transport in 2D van der Waals magnet; practical spintronic applications require higher operating temperatures.

model_io.model: CrOCl two-dimensional antiferromagnetic insulator

hypotheses.hypothesis: Flat magnonic bands induce quasi-1D transport in 2D spin lattice via reduced perpendicular diffusion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; flat b-axis band suppresses b-axis transport; dispersive a-axis band enables long-range transport

title: An ultrafast plenoptic-camera system for high-resolution 3D particle tracking in unsegmented scintillators
task: 3D particle tracking in unsegmented scintillators using plenoptic imaging
key_results: Spatial resolution of 200 micrometers achieved; full event reconstruction demonstrated for neutrino detection
comments: Novel approach replaces costly fine segmentation with single-photon sensitive plenoptic cameras, though scalability to industrial detectors remains to be demonstrated.

model_io.model: Plenoptic camera + SPAD array imaging sensor

hypotheses.hypothesis: Plenoptic systems enable 3D localization of single photons in unsegmented scintillator without dense electronics segmentation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 200 μm spatial resolution achieved in neutrino detection case study

title: Supercurrent-controlled spontaneous ferromagnetism of magnetic impurities in a spin-orbit-coupled superconductor
task: Study ferromagnetic order of magnetic impurities in spin-orbit-coupled superconductor
key_results: Spontaneous ferromagnetic domains observed in Fe(Se,Te) superconductor; magnetic switching current density three orders of magnitude lower than ferromagnetic metals
comments: Demonstrates unconventional control of ferromagnetism via supercurrents in SOC systems, though the microscopic mechanism of impurity coupling warrants further investigation.

model_io.model: Fe(Se,Te) s-wave superconductor with Rashba spin-orbit coupling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Rashba SOC enables long-range ferromagnetic order between impurity moments in s-wave superconductor
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; spontaneous magnetic domains and supercurrent-controlled ferromagnetism observed

hypotheses.hypothesis: Magneto-electric effect in Rashba superconductor generates anomalous supercurrents
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; remanent flux patterns consistent with in-plane magnetization

title: Absorption, dispersion, and emission characteristics of novel Chitosan blends thin films tuned by UV-Ozone
task: Tune optical and nonlinear properties of Chitosan blend thin films via UV-Ozone treatment
key_results: Energy gap reduced from 5.14 eV (Chitosan) to 4.0-4.25 eV (blends); fluorescence intensity decreased by UV exposure; third-order nonlinearity enhanced
comments: Comprehensive optical characterization demonstrates UV-tunability for optoelectronic devices, though performance benchmarks against commercial alternatives are missing.

model_io.model: Chitosan-derived polymer blend thin films under UV-Ozone treatment

hypotheses.hypothesis: UV-Ozone treatment reduces energy gap and enhances nonlinear optical properties in Chitosan blends
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Eg reduction and fluorescence quenching observed

title: Dynamics of a discrete-time predator-prey model with exponential prey growth and saturated response
task: Analyze bifurcations and chaos in discrete-time predator-prey model with Holling response
key_results: Period-doubling and Neimark-Sacker bifurcations identified; transitions from stability to periodic oscillations and chaos demonstrated
comments: Rigorous bifurcation analysis with numerical validation, but ecological parameter ranges and applicability to natural populations require discussion.

model_io.model: Discrete-time predator-prey system with Holling functional response and Ricker map

hypotheses.hypothesis: Holling functional response induces period-doubling and Neimark-Sacker bifurcations in discrete predator-prey model
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; central manifold theorem and bifurcation theory characterize transitions

title: On the min-entropy of equilibrium graphs in network creation games
task: Quantify structural complexity of equilibrium graphs in network creation games via min-entropy
key_results: Exact EGME characterization for edge-cost regimes α<2 and α≥2; strong correlations with graph density, diameter, and betweenness centrality
comments: EGME provides novel complexity metric complementary to classical network measures, though its predictive utility for game-theoretic outcomes needs clarification.

model_io.model: Network creation game with strategic agents

hypotheses.hypothesis: EGME based on social cost distribution effectively measures structural complexity in equilibrium networks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; EGME correlates strongly with density, diameter, and centrality metrics

title: Position tracking control of electro-hydrostatic actuators via adaptive finite-time backstepping and state observation
task: Control position tracking of electro-hydrostatic actuators under unmeasured states via adaptive backstepping
key_results: Finite-time stability guaranteed via Lyapunov analysis; state observer estimates unmeasured states; adaptive NN law handles unknown dynamics
comments: Theoretically rigorous control design combining SOB and adaptive backstepping, though experimental validation results lack quantitative performance metrics.

model_io.model: Electro-hydrostatic actuator with symmetrical cylinder + state observer + adaptive backstepping controller

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive finite-time backstepping with SOB ensures tracking performance under unmeasured states and disturbances
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Lyapunov stability and backstepping synthesis guarantee finite-time convergence

title: Evolution of tripartite entanglement in three-qubit quantum gravity-induced entanglement of masses (QGEM) with quantum decoherence
task: Study tripartite entanglement evolution in three-qubit QGEM under decoherence
key_results: Three-qubit QGEM generates genuine tripartite entanglement; three-qubit configurations more resilient to decoherence than two-qubit setups
comments: Theoretical analysis of gravitationally-induced entanglement robustness is rigorous, though experimental feasibility and parameter ranges for witness detection need practical assessment.

model_io.model: Three-qubit QGEM system with environmental decoherence

hypotheses.hypothesis: Three-qubit QGEM generates genuine tripartite entanglement resistant to decoherence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; gravitational interaction produces genuine entanglement in specified parameter regimes

title: Coupled aerodynamic–electromagnetic modeling for RCS estimation of million-scale chaff clouds with arbitrarily curved 3D geometries
task: Estimate radar cross section of large-scale chaff clouds incorporating aerodynamic evolution
key_results: Real-time RCS prediction for million-scale chaff with arbitrary 3D geometries; monostatic RCS strongly influenced by flattened/helical aerodynamic motions
comments: Novel 6-DoF aerodynamic model coupled with fast MoM solver enables efficient multiphysics simulation, though validation against field measurements remains to be reported.

model_io.model: Coupled aerodynamic-electromagnetic framework for chaff clouds

hypotheses.hypothesis: Aerodynamic effects significantly modulate RCS through orientation and spatial distribution evolution
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; monostatic RCS strongly dependent on flattened/helical dynamics; real-time first-principles prediction enabled

title: Analytical analysis of the nonlinear fractional order Pochhammer-Chree equation with power-law nonlinearity in elastic materials
task: Solve nonlinear fractional-order Pochhammer-Chree equation with power-law nonlinearity
key_results: Bright, dark, kink solitons and solitary waves derived analytically; fractional parameter χ controls soliton shape, width, and amplitude
comments: Kumar-Malik method effectively generates diverse soliton solutions, though applications to realistic elastic material parameters need demonstration.

model_io.model: Fractional Pochhammer-Chree equation with conformable fractional derivative

hypotheses.hypothesis: Kumar-Malik method yields accurate analytical solutions for fractional Pochhammer-Chree equation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; multiple soliton types derived with parameter dependence characterized

title: A novel unified lightweight temporal-spatial transformer approach for intrusion detection in drone networks
task: Detect intrusions in drone networks using lightweight temporal-spatial transformer
key_results: 99.99% multiclass detection accuracy and 100% binary anomaly detection on 2.3M records; 0.04 MB memory footprint with 9,722 parameters
comments: Exceptional performance on ISOT dataset with minimal model size, though cross-dataset generalization and robustness to adversarial attacks require evaluation.

model_io.model: TSLT-Net: Temporal-Spatial Lightweight Transformer

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-attention mechanisms effectively capture temporal and spatial dependencies for intrusion detection
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 99.99% multiclass and 100% binary accuracy achieved

title: Physics-based machine learning toolbox for probing concentration under diffusive regime in microfluidics devices
task: Predict concentration profiles in microfluidics via physics-based machine learning virtual probes
key_results: Accurate spatio-temporal concentration prediction with limited training samples; effective diffusivity and precipitation location estimates from experimental images
comments: Non-intrusive reduced basis method + U-Net architecture efficiently learns from physics-informed simulations; real-time digital twin capability demonstrated on two test geometries.

model_io.model: Non-intrusive reduced basis method (U-Net + Convolutional Autoencoder)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Physics-informed ML toolbox reduces computational cost of concentration prediction in microfluidics while maintaining accuracy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; efficient predictions on different base patterns with few training samples
